title: SKILL.md: wire roadmap guidance to tk's planning signals (action:plan, needs_planning)
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §1 + §3. File: skills/ticks/SKILL.md only.

tk now surfaces planning needs natively (shipped in phase 2):
- tk next --json returns {"action":"plan"} when the result is an unblocked childless epic (and "action":"implement" otherwise). Human output: '<id>  P<n> epic  <title>  (needs planning — no child ticks)'.
- tk graph <epic> --json returns {"needs_planning": true} for childless epics; human output explains planning vs blocked.

Update the Roadmaps section (and the Step 5/quick-reference parts that mention tk next/tk graph) so the skill text uses these signals as the structural trigger:
- When tk next returns action:plan, the move is: flesh that epic out into ticks (per the roadmap guidance + foundation-first procedure in references/tick-patterns.md), then continue with tk graph.
- When tk graph reports needs_planning, same move.
- Mention the action field exists on ALL tk next --json results (implement | plan) so orchestration can switch on it.

Keep edits surgical — this is wiring existing guidance to existing CLI behavior, not new concepts. Match the section's current tone and bash-example style.

Do NOT touch references/claude-runner.md or tick-patterns.md (other ticks own those).
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md mentions action:plan and needs_planning as the planning triggers in the Roadmaps section; quick-reference notes the action field; no contradictions with claude-runner.md
